news.title:
Oracle Financial Services and HAL are recommended as holds and buys, respectively, with positive long-term outlooks.
ONGC receives a sell call due to market risks, while Gail is attractive at current levels with a target of Rs 160-165.
news.summary:

news.title: Oracle announces global layoffs, with thousands affected in cost-cutting efforts.
news.summary: Oracle announces global layoffs, with thousands affected in cost-cutting efforts.

news.title:
Nifty IT fell 1.5% as fears of Iran's threats to subsea cables heightened risks to India's digital connectivity.
TCS rose, while Infosys, Persistent Systems, OFSS, LTIMindtree, and others declined. Analysts see volatility ahead for IT stocks.
news.summary:
Nifty IT fell 1.5% as fears of Iran's threats to subsea cables heightened risks to India's digital connectivity.
TCS rose, while Infosys, Persistent Systems, OFSS, LTIMindtree, and others declined. Analysts see volatility ahead for IT stocks.

news.title:
Nifty IT gains 1.2% as rupee hits record low; Oracle Financial leads with 5% rise.
Weaker rupee boosts IT profitability; DSP Mutual Fund highlights sector's solid ROEs, valuations.
news.summary:
Nifty IT gains 1.2% as rupee hits record low; Oracle Financial leads with 5% rise.
Weaker rupee boosts IT profitability; DSP Mutual Fund highlights sector's solid ROEs, valuations.

news.title:
Oracle Financial Services (OFSS) surged 7% on a major US bank deal, replacing existing agreements.
Despite gains, technical resistance looms near ₹7,444; broader market remains weak with Nifty 50 down 1%.
news.summary:
Oracle Financial Services (OFSS) surged 7% on a major US bank deal, replacing existing agreements.
Despite gains, technical resistance looms near ₹7,444; broader market remains weak with Nifty 50 down 1%.

news.title: Oracle Financial shares climb 6% after major order win.
news.summary: Oracle Financial shares climb 6% after major order win.

news.title:
Oracle Financial signs a deal with a US global bank for perpetual software licensing.
The deal, valued at $100M (₹940 crore), includes transition services and personnel.
news.summary:

news.title:
Oracle Financial Services surged 5.3% intraday, hitting Rs 6,645 on 18 Mar 2026.
The stock outperformed its sector and benchmark indices significantly.
news.summary:

news.title:
Oracle shares rose 8% after strong earnings and guidance amid market volatility.
Brent oil fell 7.8% to $91.2; S&P 500 dropped 0.21%, 10-yr Treasury yield at 4.15%.

news.title:
Oracle Financial Services' Q3 net profit rose to ₹6.1B from ₹5.4B YoY.
Q3 revenue increased to ₹19.66B, up from ₹17.15B YoY.
news.summary:

news.title:
Oracle Financial Services Software Ltd's board meeting on Jan 21, 2026, will review Q3 and nine-month financial results.
The unaudited standalone and consolidated results for Dec 31, 2025, will also be published on the company's website.
news.summary:
Oracle Financial Services Software Ltd's board meeting on Jan 21, 2026, will review Q3 and nine-month financial results.
The unaudited standalone and consolidated results for Dec 31, 2025, will also be published on the company's website.

news.title:
FPIs sold $8.5B of Indian IT stocks in 2025, the highest annual selloff, as Nifty IT Index fell 13% vs Nifty 50's 10.5% gain.
Oracle fell 40%, TCS 21%, HCLTech 15% amid H-1B visa concerns. Telecom stocks saw $5.4B inflows, while FMCG faced $4.2B outflows.
news.summary:
FPIs sold $8.5B of Indian IT stocks in 2025, the highest annual selloff, as Nifty IT Index fell 13% vs Nifty 50's 10.5% gain.
Oracle fell 40%, TCS 21%, HCLTech 15% amid H-1B visa concerns. Telecom stocks saw $5.4B inflows, while FMCG faced $4.2B outflows.

news.title:
Nifty IT index fell 2.5% on January 5 amid expectations of muted Q3 FY26 growth.
IT sector stocks like Infosys, TCS, and Wipro faced pressure during the session.
news.summary:
